title: Stamped hooks break under host "type":"module" — ship template hooks as .cjs
description: All hooks in src/template/.claude/hooks/ are CommonJS .js files. When a host project's package.json declares "type":"module" (observed live in evo-gnit), Node treats every .js hook as ESM and each throws ReferenceError: require is not defined in ES module scope — gate-watch, spawn-guard, message-nudge, viewer-server, load-report, hook-lib, and every rules file silently or visibly fail. Rename the stamped hook and rules files to .cjs, update intra-hook require paths (e.g. require('./hook-lib') → './hook-lib.cjs'), and update every hook command in src/template/.claude/settings.json to point at the .cjs filenames. The Scaffolder must stamp the .cjs layout for new Instances.
acceptance_criteria: Every file in src/template/.claude/hooks/ uses the .cjs extension; no .js hook or rules file remains in the template.
All intra-hook require() paths resolve to the .cjs filenames.
src/template/.claude/settings.json hook commands reference the .cjs paths.
A scaffold stamped into a temp project whose package.json has "type":"module" runs every hook (PostToolUse, Stop, spawn-guard, viewer-server) without an ESM ReferenceError.
package-qa stamp-contract check passes on the new manifest.
root_cause: Stamped hooks were CommonJS .js; host package.json "type":"module" made Node parse them as ESM, so require() threw.
fix: Template hooks renamed to .cjs with settings.json/requires/health-policy updated; landed in commit c624700 after code-standards and package-qa PASS.
